opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I've suddenly got a pain in my right calf and I'm terrified I'm getting a blood clot — I'm 9 weeks pregnant and I know I'm high risk.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on a couple of hours ago and I can't stop thinking about a clot travelling to my lungs. I couldn't wait until tomorrow.

symptom_narrative.character: started as a dull ache, then a sharp cramp when I walk
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: maybe 5 out of 10 when I walk on it
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse when I walk, eases when I rest
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no swelling or redness that I can see, no chest symptoms
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I had one early miscarriage before; that's when they found the antibodies
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm resting in bed, I can still walk but it's sore

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a clotting antibody problem they found after my miscarriage
past_medical_history.how_managed: they put me on a low-dose aspirin in pregnancy

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: very anxious about the pregnancy after the previous loss

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told the baby and she are safe

access_barriers.transport: partner at home with a car

objective_data_offered.value: right calf looks the same as the left on camera, no obvious redness or swelling that I can see
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-assessment on video; cannot reliably measure calf circumference or detect early DVT
